title: High-Grade Borrowers Jump Back Into Bond Market After Halt
description: US investment-grade bond market reopens after three-session pause amid easing Iran conflict concerns. Six firms raise $7 billion in debt offerings following presidential de-escalation announcement, signaling reduced geopolitical risk premium.

title: Oakbridge Names New President and CEO as Smith Moves to Chairman Role
description: Oakbridge Insurance announces leadership transition with longtime CEO transitioning to executive chairman role and CFO assuming CEO position effective July 2025, reflecting organizational succession planning.

title: EPA Promises Ruling on New Hampshire Repeal of Vehicle Inspections by Summer
description: EPA Administrator commits to expedited review of New Hampshire's Clean Air Act waiver request regarding vehicle inspection program repeal, impacting environmental compliance and associated insurance requirements.

title: Mosaic Insurance reorganises underwriting hubs to support global growth
description: Mosaic Insurance restructures international underwriting hubs to optimize global growth strategy. Singapore operations leadership change announced as part of broader resource realignment and international expansion initiative.

title: Fire Destroys Historic Courthouse in Georgia
description: Historic courthouse destroyed by fire in Georgia, displacing government operations. Property loss claim implications for public entity liability and business interruption coverage in U.S. domestic market.

title: Amiga Specialty partners with AXA XL to bolster Financial Institutions offering
description: Amiga Specialty MGA expands financial institutions capacity through AXA XL partnership, strengthening core FI risk coverage globally. Strategic reinsurance and underwriting growth for specialist insurers.

title: Florida Peninsula targets $150m named storm reinsurance with Palm Re 2026-1 cat bond
description: Florida Peninsula secures $150m named storm catastrophe bond through third Palm Re Ltd sponsorship, demonstrating continued reliance on cat bond markets for hurricane reinsurance capacity and risk transfer.

title: Nephila Capital’s flagship Syndicate 2357 reports 48% higher profit for 2025
description: Nephila Capital's Lloyd's Syndicate 2357 reports 48% profit increase for 2025, reflecting strong insurance-linked securities and catastrophe market performance benefiting dedicated ILS managers.

title: Ariel Re makes key promotions in Bermuda & London, including Kerr to SVP, Capital
description: Ariel Re announces senior management promotions including Andy Kerr to SVP Capital across Bermuda and London offices, reflecting organizational growth in reinsurance capital management.

title: Blending public and private ILS essential to moderate structural trade-offs: Schroders Capital
description: Schroders Capital analysis highlights necessity of blending public and private ILS to optimize structural trade-offs between risk-adjusted returns and liquidity in insurance-linked securities.

title: Pool Re secures £100m of terrorism retro from fourth Baltic PCC catastrophe bond
description: Pool Re secures £100m terrorism retrocession via fourth Baltic PCC catastrophe bond, expanding UK government-backed mutual's capacity to manage terrorism risk across British insurance market.

title: FCC Bans Wireless Router Imports, Citing Security Concerns
description: FCC imposes ban on foreign wireless router imports citing national security threats, creating potential product liability and cyber risk implications for technology insurers and underwriters.

title: Mike Lynch’s Estate Refused Permission to Appeal in UK HP-Autonomy Case
description: UK High Court denies appeal by Mike Lynch's estate in HP-Autonomy case, confirming liability ruling affecting technology M&A insurance claims and representations/warranties coverage outcomes.

title: German Court Rejects Bid to Ban Mercedes and BMW’s Fossil-Fuel Cars
description: German federal court rejects environmental lawsuits seeking fossil-fuel combustion vehicle ban, reducing near-term regulatory risk for auto manufacturers but highlighting climate policy uncertainty.

title: Hyundai Recalls 58,000 Palisade SUVs in South Korea Over Automatic Seat Problem
description: Hyundai recalls 58,000 Palisade SUVs in South Korea following fatal accident due to automatic seat defects, triggering product liability and warranty claims exposure across vehicle fleet.

title: Iran Sends Waves of Missiles Into Israel, Calls Trump’s Talk of Negotiations ‘Fake News’
description: Iran launches multiple missile waves targeting Israel amidst diplomatic tensions, escalating Middle East geopolitical volatility and triggering war risk, terrorism cover, and energy infrastructure exposures.

title: Turning Claims Documents into Decisions
description: Wisedocs launches AI-powered claims decision platform addressing nuclear verdicts and litigation funding pressures. Solution targets carriers, TPAs, and legal teams seeking faster file closure and improved reserve adequacy through automated document analysis.

title: Loss Trends Outpacing Pricing Assumptions: Other Liability Analysis
description: U.S. other liability (occurrence) line experienced $7.3 billion adverse loss development in 2025, with over 50% from recent accident years. Significant indicator of social inflation and loss trending exceeding underwriting assumptions across recent cohorts.

title: Trump’s Demand for Admissions Data Sends Wary Colleges Scrambling
description: Trump administration demands comprehensive college admissions data amid regulatory uncertainty. Potential insurance implications for educators' liability, D&O, and employment practices coverage as diversity program scrutiny intensifies.

title: NY Lawmakers Urged to Have Faith in Auto Insurance Reform Numbers. But Do They?
description: New York auto insurance reform gaining broad stakeholder support including insurers, businesses, and unions. Regulatory environment shifting toward reform addressing competitive distortions and pricing inadequacy in auto liability segment.

title: Alphabet’s Wing to Start Drone Delivery in San Francisco Bay Area
description: Alphabet's Wing expanding drone delivery operations into San Francisco Bay Area. Emerging autonomous delivery exposure requiring new underwriting frameworks for product liability, cyber, and operational risk in uncrewed aircraft systems.

title: ‘Perfect time’ for climate-tech focus, says Dickson as OAK Global unveils new unit
description: OAK Global establishes OAK Horizon unit targeting interconnected climate-technology risks. Reinsurer positioning capital toward emerging exposures amid narrowing intervention window for climate volatility across multiple sectors.

title: HDI Global’s revenue increases to €10.3bn in 2025
description: HDI Global achieves 5% revenue growth to €10.3 billion in 2025, driven by new business and inflation-adjusted pricing. Strong underwriting fundamentals and pricing discipline in corporate specialty lines supporting growth trajectory.

title: Gallagher Re report warns traditional insurance is failing to cover AI-related liabilities
description: Gallagher Re white paper warns traditional insurance frameworks inadequately address AI-related liabilities. Significant coverage gap identified across existing policies as artificial intelligence deployment accelerates among clients.

title: Alacrity Solutions establishes UK presence to support Lloyd’s-placed US risks
description: Alacrity Solutions establishes Lloyd's presence specializing in complex U.S. catastrophe claims servicing. Strategic positioning enhances claims management capacity for London Market insurers placing American casualty and CAT exposures.

title: AM Best maintains stable outlook on UK non-life insurance segment
description: AM Best maintains stable outlook on UK non-life segment despite elevated geopolitical risks. Balanced headwinds and tailwinds offset sector vulnerabilities including minimal growth and employment uncertainty impacting underwriting results.

title: Ariel Re rolls out promotions across its Bermuda and London offices
description: Ariel Re advances talent retention through management promotions across Bermuda and London offices, reinforcing institutional focus on professional development and career progression within global reinsurance operations.

title: Brit launches cargo consortium with capacity of up to $80m
description: Brit Group launches BRIDGE cargo consortium with $80m capacity, expanding specialty cargo insurance offerings and addressing broker-client coverage gaps in competitive marketplace.

title: Chaucer and Ceto partner to launch new marine MGA
description: Chaucer and Ceto AI establish Lloyd's-regulated marine MGA, combining predictive analytics with marine hull underwriting to enhance risk assessment and binding authority in specialty maritime insurance.

title: OAK Global launches Horizon unit to target climate and technology risks
description: OAK Global establishes OAK Horizon strategic business unit to underwrite climate and technology risk intersections, leveraging existing Lloyd's syndicate capacity for cross-class opportunities.

title: Peak Re appoints Swiss Re’s Victor Kuk as CEO, Franz-Josef Hahn transitions to Special Advisor
description: Peak Re appoints Victor Kuk from Swiss Re as CEO, succeeding founder Franz-Josef Hahn who assumes advisory role. Leadership transition reflects strategic evolution in Hong Kong-based global reinsurance operations.

title: The Tennessee Department of Commerce & Insurance reports sixth straight year of captive insurance expansion
description: Tennessee captive insurance market continues expansion for sixth consecutive year, with growth in both active cells and risk-bearing entities. Reflects broader U.S. alternative risk transfer market strengthening and regulatory support.

title: MSIG USA names Matt Thompson as South-Central Region Distribution Leader
description: MSIG USA appoints Matt Thompson as South-Central Region Distribution Leader, overseeing commercial and personal insurance operations across Texas, Oklahoma, Mississippi, Louisiana, and Arkansas to drive regional growth and strategic market initiatives.

title: Register: Risky Future AI Tools for Better Billing & Payments ‘Demo Day’ on March 25
description: Insurance Journal hosts AI demonstration event for billing and payments solutions. Targets carriers, retail agencies, MGAs and finance operations professionals seeking automation tools for operational efficiency.

title: Wildfires, Storms, Floods Account for Record 92% of Global Insured Losses: Swiss Re
description: Secondary perils—wildfires, storms, floods—drove record 92% of $107bn global catastrophe insured losses in 2025. Swiss Re data indicates escalating climate-related exposure and claims concentration risk for underwriters.

title: Insurance sector needs hybrid talent amid rapid automation: Aon’s Blain
description: Aon analysis reveals 97% of insurers accelerating automation adoption. Identifies hybrid workforce talent strategies as competitive necessity amid technology-driven industry transformation and talent market redefinition.

title: Coca-Cola pension fund ILS investment grew to $266m on returns in 2025
description: Coca-Cola's pension fund ILS portfolio expanded to $266m in 2025, reflecting strong returns in insurance-linked securities. Demonstrates institutional appetite for alternative risk transfer mechanisms and cat bond performance.

title: Hawaii’s Worst Flooding in 20 Years Prompts Evacuations
description: Hawaii experiences worst flooding in two decades following saturated soil conditions and sequential heavy rainfall events. Significant claims exposure for property, casualty, and business interruption coverage across the state.

title: Relation Insurance Acquires Chinook Insurance Group in Washington
description: Relation Insurance acquires Chinook Insurance Group's subsea and aquaculture operations in Seattle. Consolidation strengthens specialist marine and offshore energy risk underwriting capacity in North American market.

title: Nebraska Fires Burn Grazing Lands, Threaten Plans to Grow US Cattle Herd
description: Severe Nebraska wildfires destroy extensive grazing lands threatening US cattle production expansion. Agricultural and livestock underwriters face elevated exposure; commodity price inflation impacts farm property and business interruption claims.

title: Florida Man Faked Brain Injury for Years in Attempt to Gain $6M in Insurance
description: Multi-year auto insurance fraud scheme involving fabricated brain injury claims seeking $6m damages. Highlights organized fraud rings and claims investigation vulnerabilities in personal lines coverage.

title: P/C Industry Loss Reserves Redundant by More Than $20B: Assured Research
description: US P&C industry year-end 2025 loss reserves redundant by $20bn-plus, indicating conservative reserving and favorable prior-year loss development. Positive signals for underwriting profitability and reserve adequacy.

title: Chubb appoints new heads of Property and Casualty in Overseas General division
description: Chubb announces senior leadership appointments in Overseas General division commercial property and casualty operations. Organizational restructuring reflects strategic emphasis on specialty lines growth.

title: Starr completes IQUW deal, strengthens specialty re/insurance presence
description: Starr completes IQUW acquisition, consolidating specialty reinsurance and insurance platform across London, Bermuda and UK retail motor markets. Significant market consolidation enhancing specialty underwriting capabilities.

title: USAA adding to stated reinsurance for some ResRe cat bonds after SCS model update
description: USAA enhances reinsurance structure for residential catastrophe bonds by adding retained quota share layer following SCS model recalibration, affecting aggregate coverage terms.

title: NormanMax targets Latin & South America growth with Alvaro Espinel Pardo hire
description: NormanMax appoints regional VP to drive parametric insurance expansion across Latin and South American markets, strengthening Lloyd's syndicate distribution capabilities.

title: Trump Suit Against Capital One Dismissed But Can Be Refiled
description: Trump Organization's lawsuit against Capital One dismissed for procedural deficiencies but permitted refiling; addresses account closure dispute with potential reputational implications.

title: Planned Parenthood of Illinois to Pay $500K to End DEI-Related Investigation
description: Planned Parenthood Illinois settles federal discrimination investigation for $500K over alleged employee segregation and harassment, establishing employment practice liability precedent.

title: Frustrated Corpus Christi Residents Limit Water Use as Crisis Nears
description: Corpus Christi water crisis escalates as residents enforce usage restrictions; emerging drought-related property and business interruption risk exposure in Texas.

title: Chubb Outlines Structure of $20B Gulf Reinsurance Facility, Now Including Liability Cover
description: Chubb launches $20 billion Gulf maritime reinsurance facility covering war risk for Strait of Hormuz shipping; expanded scope now includes liability coverage.

title: People Moves: MSIG USA Appoints Thompson as South Central Distribution Leader
description: MSIG USA appoints regional distribution leader for South Central operations, signalling expansion strategy across five-state territory including Texas and Louisiana.

title: Harvard Sued by Justice Department Over Alleged Antisemitism on Campus
description: Justice Department sues Harvard University alleging campus antisemitism and civil rights violations; escalates institutional liability exposure and E&O claim risk.

title: Southwest Heat Wave Tumbles Records, Raises Fire Risk
description: Southwest heat wave with record temperatures threatens 9.5 million residents; elevated wildfire risk across multiple states demands CAT modeling updates and claims preparation.

title: Starr Completes Acquisition of IQUW Group
description: Starr completes IQUW Group acquisition, establishing diversified specialty reinsurance platform with enhanced London market, Bermuda and UK retail motor capabilities.

title: Two Pilots Killed, Dozens Injured in Jet Collision With Fire Truck at LaGuardia Airport
description: Air Canada Express CRJ-900 collided with fire truck during LaGuardia landing, killing both pilots and injuring dozens. Incident closed major airport facility, triggering significant aviation liability and property damage claims exposure.

title: Musk Found Liable to Twitter Shareholders in Fraud Lawsuit Over Takeover
description: U.S. federal jury found Elon Musk liable for shareholder fraud claims related to Twitter takeover. Decision carries implications for D&O liability, transaction risk underwriting, and corporate governance insurance exposure.

title: Trump Puts Off Threat to Bomb Iran Power Grid; Iran Denies There Are Cease Fire Talks
description: Trump postpones threatened military strikes against Iranian power infrastructure for five days amid ongoing conflict escalation. War risk exclusions and political violence coverage remain critical underwriting considerations for MENA operations.

title: Scott Kreuzer joins PartnerRe as CEO P&C Americas

title: Everest to sell Canadian Retail Insurance operations to Wawanesa
description: Everest Group sells Canadian retail insurance operations to Wawanesa Mutual Insurance. Transaction reflects portfolio optimization strategy and market consolidation within North American specialty insurance segment.

title: Berkley Insurance Asia announces key leadership moves within its Marine team
description: Berkley Insurance Asia appoints Kenneth Cheong as Head of Marine Asia, effective April 2026. Leadership restructuring within marine team indicates strategic realignment and expansion priorities in Asian maritime insurance markets.

title: Prolonged Middle East conflict could stretch re/insurers’ loss exposure despite exclusions
description: Autonomous report warns that prolonged Middle East conflict could expand re/insurer loss exposure despite war exclusions. Tail risk of territorial escalation and contract coverage gaps presents significant claims severity potential for underwriters.

title: Peter Babej joins Reinsurance Group of America’s Board of Directors
description: RGA appoints Peter Babej to Board of Directors effective April 2026. Former Citigroup banking executive brings financial services expertise to life and health reinsurance governance and strategic oversight.

title: NormanMax hires Blue Marble’s Alvaro Espinel Pardo to lead strategy & development in Latin & South America
description: NormanMax hires Alvaro Espinel Pardo as VP leading strategy and development for Latin and South America. Parametric insurer expands regional infrastructure and leadership capability in emerging market specialty insurance.

title: Fabienne Marsoner joins Gallagher Re as Broker, APAC Casualty FAC
description: Gallagher Re appoints Fabienne Marsoner as APAC Casualty FAC Broker, recruiting from Marsh's international casualty division. Appointment strengthens reinsurance broker's casualty facultative capabilities across Asia-Pacific markets.

title: Berkshire Hathaway to Invest $1.8 Billion in Tokio Marine
description: Berkshire Hathaway's National Indemnity subsidiary acquires 2.49% strategic stake in Tokio Marine Holdings for $1.8 billion, significantly expanding US conglomerate's Japanese insurance market exposure and reinsurance capacity.

description: Approximately 90 ships crossed Strait of Hormuz during Iranian oil export activity despite ongoing regional conflict. Indicates sustained shipping volumes and marine insurance activity.

description: Ninety vessels transited Strait of Hormuz amid Iranian oil exports and regional conflict. Persistent traffic validates ongoing marine war risk exposure and claims frequency assessment needs.

description: Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary National Indemnity Company acquires strategic 2.49% stake in Japanese insurer Tokio Marine for $1.8 billion, expanding U.S. reinsurance exposure to Asia-Pacific markets and demonstrating consolidation appetite.

description: Specialty reinsurer Compre completes £250 million adverse development cover transaction with European counterparty covering diversified pan-European liability reserves, demonstrating continued appetite for legacy portfolio risk transfer.

description: Berkshire Hathaway's National Indemnity establishes comprehensive strategic partnership with Tokio Marine, acquiring 2.5% equity stake as part of multi-element alliance strengthening reinsurance and capital market positioning in Japan.

description: European UCITS regulatory proposal to restrict retail investor access to catastrophe bonds faces industry opposition from specialized hedge funds managing insurance-linked strategies, threatening market structure.

description: Olympus Insurance Company upsizes debut catastrophe bond sponsorship through Abacab Re Ltd., targeting $100-150 million in multi-peril reinsurance capacity for Florida homeowners exposure.

description: Hannover Re's Kaith Re transformer vehicle issues $20 million private catastrophe bond via Seaside Re structure, demonstrating continued alternative risk transfer utilization by major reinsurers.

description: Weekly compilation of top catastrophe bond, ILS, and reinsurance capital market developments providing market intelligence on risk transfer innovations and capacity trends.

title: CRC Group leverages ILS capacity to support data center capabilities
description: CRC Group deploys ILS market capacity to support emerging data center insurance capabilities, reflecting expansion of alternative risk transfer beyond traditional property catastrophe exposures.

title: Latest US SCS outbreak to become March’s second $1bn event: Gallagher Re
description: March 15-16 severe convective storm outbreak in central and eastern US estimated at $1 billion, marking second major SCS loss event for month; significant claims implications for property underwriters.

description: Leadenhall analysis demonstrates catastrophe bonds and ILS exhibit lower volatility than broader financial markets during geopolitical stress, supporting diversification thesis for institutional capital.

description: One Alliance North America pursues inaugural $100 million+ multi-peril catastrophe bond issuance, expanding sponsor universe and demonstrating continued retail insurance sector access to ILS markets.

description: Pool Re launches fourth terrorism catastrophe bond seeking £100 million retroactive capacity, demonstrating UK government-backed mutual's continued reliance on ILS for terrorism exposure management.

description: Aon appoints Dan Tomlinson as Head of Alternative Risk Transfer Solutions for EMEA, signaling continued investment in structured insurance-linked securities and risk transfer advisory capabilities.

description: Hiscox Capital Partners consolidates two decades of capital partnership activity under unified platform, representing strategic evolution toward streamlined third-party capital management and deployment.

description: Tokio Marine Holdings enters strategic partnership with Berkshire Hathaway, selling 2.49% stake via treasury share allotment. Significant capital restructuring and potential reinsurance implications for global insurance markets.

description: Ping An's overseas investment arm reassessing US exposure amid Iran conflict volatility. Geopolitical tensions triggering portfolio rebalancing across major Asian insurers' international assets.

description: Iranian government-linked hackers restore website after US domain seizure. Ongoing cyber threat activity highlights persistent state-sponsored cyber risk to healthcare and critical infrastructure sectors.

title: Hyundai Says More Injuries Tied to SUV Rear Power Seat Recall
description: Hyundai Palisade power seat recalls expanded with additional injury reports. Product liability claims accumulation and potential systematic manufacturing defect driving significant auto insurance loss exposure.

title: Remote Spanish Airport Again Becomes Parking Lot for Planes, This Time on Iran War
description: Remote Spanish airport serves as aircraft parking lot due to Iran war airspace closures. Escalating geopolitical disruption to aviation operations creating stranded aircraft, delay, and contingency claims.

title: The Luxury Life of British Expats in Dubai Faces a Reality Check
description: British expat lifestyle in Dubai facing economic headwinds. Regional economic volatility and potential expatriate repatriation trends affecting property, life, and specialty insurance demand in UAE.

title: ‘Mental Health Trip’ to NY Not Compensable, Florida Appellate Court Finds
description: Florida appellate court denies workers' compensation coverage for quad-disabled worker's mental health travel. Legal precedent narrowing compensable injury-related expenses and travel benefit exposure.

title: Predicting the Insurability of Prediction Markets
description: Analysis of insurability frameworks for prediction markets and novelty wagering platforms. Emerging non-traditional risk classes and coverage uncertainties in speculative markets requiring underwriting reassessment.

title: Orlando Apartment Complex Evacuated After Cracks Found on All Five Floors
description: Orlando apartment complex evacuation following structural cracks across all five floors. Significant property damage and potential casualty exposure from structural defects affecting multi-unit residential portfolios.

title: Negligence Suit Filed Over Death of Former New York Yankee’s Teenage Son at Resort
description: Negligence lawsuit filed over carbon dioxide-related death at Costa Rica resort. Premises liability and hospitality sector claims highlighting inadequate safety protocols and potential policy coverage disputes.

title: Ohio Bill Would Establish Online Auto Insurance Verification
description: Ohio legislature proposes online auto insurance verification system to enforce compliance and reduce uninsured driver exposure. House Bill 678 aims to improve underwriting controls and claims cost mitigation through mandatory digital verification protocols.

title: Nebraska AG Sues Roblox Over Alleged Child Safety Failures
description: Nebraska AG pursues consumer protection lawsuit against Roblox alleging inadequate child safety controls exposing minors to predatory conduct. Case raises product liability and duty-of-care underwriting considerations for digital platform operators.

title: Meta Loses Insurance for Defense in Major Social Media Addiction Litigation
description: Delaware court rules insurers have no duty to defend Meta in thousands of child addiction lawsuits, eliminating coverage for social media harm claims. Decision impacts cyber/tech liability underwriting and D&O policy scope across digital platforms.

title: Rise of the Analytical Agent: Building Stronger Insurance Agencies with Business Intelligence
description: Insurance agencies adopt business intelligence and data analytics tools to manage operational complexity and market uncertainty. Technology adoption critical for agency competitiveness in underwriting, claims, and customer retention strategies.

title: Carriers Using AI for Claims but Adoption Is Fragmented, Report Shows
description: Insurance carriers show uneven AI adoption in claims operations with limited scale deployment. Fragmented technology implementation creates operational risk and competitive pressure across underwriting and claims processing workflows.

title: PartnerRe posts rise in net income to $2.1bn for FY’25
description: PartnerRe reports FY2025 net income of $2.1bn, up 46% from prior year, with non-life underwriting profit of $364mn. Strong reinsurance market performance reflects improved premium adequacy and favorable claims experience in non-life segment.

title: US life insurance premiums hit record $17.5bn in 2025: LIMRA
description: US individual life insurance premium sales reach record $17.5bn in 2025, establishing new industry benchmark. Growth signals strong market conditions and improved underwriting demand in life segment with favorable mortality assumptions.

title: QBE expands transactional liability in Asia with Reuben Lee appointment
description: QBE appoints dedicated Transactional Liability Lead for Asia to drive M&A underwriting growth and market expansion. Organizational restructuring signals strategic focus on merger/acquisition risk underwriting and portfolio diversification in high-growth region.

title: IGI adds former Guy Carpenter MD Thomas A. Collett to its Board
description: IGI appoints former Guy Carpenter managing director Thomas Collett to Board, strengthening reinsurance and risk management governance. Leadership addition reflects strategic repositioning for specialist commercial and reinsurance portfolio development.

description: Intelligent AI integrates high-resolution property risk and rebuild cost data with Guidewire platform for enhanced underwriting decisioning. Technology partnership improves claims estimation accuracy and property risk assessment across InsuranceSuite workflows.

description: Chubb named lead underwriter for DFC's $20bn Gulf maritime reinsurance facility. Structure details outlined for this major US-backed capacity initiative supporting regional maritime risk underwriting and claims management.

description: Balavant Insurance launches Sentric Specialty program manager delivering specialty solutions across manufacturing, crisis management, and professional liability risks via Hudson-backed platform.

description: Tokio Marine Kiln expands Aerospace division hiring Cameron Glover as Aviation Underwriter focusing on Airport and Product liability risks, strengthening aviation specialty underwriting capacity.

description: Guy Carpenter's Asia Pacific CEO outlines terrorism risk resilience through reinsurance pools and retrocession mechanisms addressing complex evolving threat landscape and market capacity constraints.

description: Strait of Hormuz vessel insurance premiums escalate to approximately 5% of ship value following maritime attacks. Coverage remains available but at significantly elevated war risk rates, reflecting heightened geopolitical tension and underwriter exposure reassessment.

description: Nexus Underwriting appoints senior financial institutions head for UK portfolio management. Senior hire signals capacity expansion and strategic focus on financial lines underwriting and portfolio growth in UK market.

description: Willis partners with Qover on embedded insurance technology platform expansion across GB affinity ecosystem. Strategic alliance integrates digital orchestration capabilities to enhance product distribution and customer access.

description: Pacific Life Re appoints experienced protection reinsurance executive as Managing VP for business services. Senior hire brings 25+ years Asia-Pacific life insurance expertise to enhance life reinsurance underwriting and strategy.

title: Opinion | Iran war shows urgency of Hong Kong green shipping transition
description: Opinion piece discussing Iran conflict implications for Hong Kong's green shipping transition. Addresses geopolitical risk impacts on maritime sustainability initiatives and potential operational disruptions in regional shipping corridors.

title: Amazon Unit Withdraws From Drone Trade Group, Raises Safety Concerns
description: Amazon Prime Air exits Commercial Drone Alliance over regulatory disagreement on detect-and-avoid collision systems. Safety standards divergence signals emerging underwriting challenges for unmanned aircraft liability and product liability exposures.

description: Iranian-linked hackers escalating cyberattacks targeting US defense contractors, power infrastructure and water utilities. Elevated cyber risk exposure for critical infrastructure insureds and network security coverage demand in MENA and North America regions.

description: HW Kaufman Group appoints new president at RB Jones specialty MGU. Routine personnel transition at specialty underwriting platform serving brokers and wholesalers across casualty and risk coverage segments.

description: AI hyperscalers utilizing off-balance-sheet debt arrangements increasing insurer and private credit fund exposure. BIS flags shadow financing risks for underwriters with technology infrastructure and portfolio concentration exposures.

description: New Jersey enforces $7M settlement against PDX North for worker misclassification and delivery driver wage violations. Regulatory enforcement action underscores evolving compliance risks in gig economy logistics and workers' compensation coverage.

title: Owners of Large California Tow Operation Charged in $6M Workers’ Comp Fraud Scheme
description: Southern California towing company owners arrested for $6M workers' compensation fraud via payroll underreporting and cash wage schemes. Criminal prosecution highlights claims fraud exposure and underwriting control gaps in regional transportation sectors.

title: Bank of America Agrees to Settle Claims It Aided Epstein
description: Bank of America settles proposed class action on Epstein sex-trafficking liability claims. Settlement agreement reflects institutional liability exposure and reputational risk management in financial services sector third-party facilitation claims.

title: GIC Re Withdraws Marine Hull War Cover
description: GIC Re, India's reinsurer, withdraws marine hull war coverage amid escalating Gulf tensions. Significant market signal indicating insurers reassessing war risk exposure in conflict-affected shipping corridors.

title: হরমুজ প্রণালীতে হামলা এড়াতে যে নতুন কৌশল জাহাজগুলোর |
description: Ships deploying new evasion strategies to avoid attacks in Strait of Hormuz. Operational changes reflect growing maritime security threats impacting vessel routing and insurance underwriting considerations.

title: Maritime insurers cancel war risk cover in Gulf as Iran conflict disrupts shipping
description: Maritime insurers canceling war risk cover across Gulf region as Iran conflict intensifies. Critical market development signaling insurers withdrawing capacity and raising premium risk for cargo and hull policies.

title: West Asia tensions : About marine insurance , the piece of paper that protects oil shipments via Strait of Hormuz
description: Analysis of marine insurance role protecting oil shipments through Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia tensions. Examines coverage mechanisms critical to tanker operations and trade finance in escalating conflict environment.

title: Iran - Krieg :  Nicht unser Krieg : EU schließt Einsatz vor Irans Küste aus
description: EU excludes military deployment near Iranian coast from conflict response. Policy decision affecting maritime risk assessment and insurance implications for European-flagged vessels and regional trade operations.

title: Iran conflict disrupts global shipping as tankers are stranded - Newspaper
description: Iran conflict causes global shipping disruption with tankers stranded amid transit restrictions. Supply chain and operational exposure creating significant claims and coverage challenges for marine and cargo insurers worldwide.

title: إغلاق مضيق هرمز : كيف تهدد الحرب الأمريكية الإسرائيلية مع إيران حركة التجارة في العالم ؟
description: Analysis of potential Strait of Hormuz closure threat from US-Israel-Iran conflict impacting global trade flows. Examines catastrophic scenarios for shipping insurance and energy supply chain risk management across markets.

title: هل يغلق الحوثيون منافذ الخليج ؟
description: Examines Houthi threat to Gulf maritime access and potential blockade scenarios. Assesses shipping corridor closure risk and implications for marine insurance capacity, war risk covers, and regional trade routes.

title: حساسية هرمز
description: Hormuz Strait sensitivity analysis amid regional tensions. Focused assessment of critical chokepoint vulnerability and downstream insurance market impacts on coverage availability and pricing for transit risk.

title: Iran war has blocked the Strait of Hormuz . Reopening it is challenge
description: Iran war scenario analysis examining Strait of Hormuz closure consequences and reopening challenges. Strategic assessment of catastrophic trade disruption implications for marine insurance claims and underwriting exposure.

title: Iran Conflict : Middle East tensions : Global insurers exit Iranian waters as conflict deepens
description: Global insurers withdrawing from Iranian waters amid escalating regional conflict. Significant capacity reduction in high-risk MENA maritime operations threatens underwriting availability and premium adequacy for affected trades.

title: Iran war has blocked the Strait of Hormuz , a vital oil choke point . Reopening it is a big challenge
description: Strait of Hormuz blockade by Iran conflict creates critical oil transit disruption affecting 20% global supply flows. Insurance implications include war risk exposure, cargo delays, and potential energy market volatility impacting P&I and cargo portfolios.

title: Basis risk a manageable, programmable feature of parametric triggers: Research
description: Research demonstrates basis risk in parametric insurance triggers is a manageable and structural feature, enabling better risk quantification for underwriters and reinsurers deploying parametric solutions.

title: One of BAM’s Fidus Re financial guarantee ILS gets ratings revised to Positive by KBRA
description: Fidus Re insurance-linked security sponsored by Build America Mutual receives ratings upgrade to Positive from KBRA, signalling improved credit quality and potential yield compression for similar ILS instruments.

title: State Farm sets up another Merna Re structure in Bermuda, likely for future cat bonds
description: State Farm establishes new Merna Re Bermuda structure, consistent with prior catastrophe bond issuance strategy, indicating continued capital market access for US primary insurers managing tail risk.

title: Industry index cat bonds outperformed market post-hurricanes Ian and Irma: Neuberger Berman
description: Industry index catastrophe bonds outperformed broader cat bond market post-hurricanes Ian and Irma, demonstrating superior loss performance for parametric-indexed structures versus traditional cat bond vehicles.

title: Hannover Re’s securitised reinsurance and ILS collateral volumes grew in 2025
description: Hannover Re expands securitised reinsurance and ILS collateral volumes in 2025, reinforcing its role as major ILS market facilitator and demonstrating strong institutional appetite for alternative risk transfer.

title: SI Re hires Schulz as ILS Analyst. CUO Salzmann now departed
description: Signal Iduna Reinsurance strengthens ILS team with new analyst hire, reflecting continued strategic focus on insurance-linked securities for portfolio diversification and enhanced return generation.

title: Heritage secures $250m Citrus Re 2026-1 named storm cat bond at reduced pricing
description: Heritage Insurance secures $250m Citrus Re 2026-1 named storm catastrophe bond at improved pricing, reflecting favourable cat bond market conditions and reduced risk premium for US property insurers.

title: Best of Artemis, week ending March 15th 2026
description: Market digest covering top ten articles on catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and alternative risk transfer mechanisms, providing comprehensive intelligence on insurance-linked securities market trends.

title: FloodSmart Re cat bonds extended further, as NFIP’s Helene loss remains above attachments
description: FloodSmart Re cat bonds extended as NFIP's Hurricane Helene losses exceed attachment levels, highlighting basis risk and potential refinancing pressures for parametric flood insurance structures.

title: Price Forbes Re hires Artex’s Mike Ward as VP Capital Markets
description: Price Forbes Capital Markets hires experienced capital markets specialist from Artex Risk Solutions, reflecting competitive talent dynamics in ILS advisory and reinsurance capital raising sectors.

title: Even Low-Risk Homes Are Caught Up in California’s Climate Insurance Crisis
description: California insurers continue market exit amid wildfire risk, forcing homeowners toward state insurer of last resort. Climate-driven underwriting contraction creates coverage gaps and affordability pressures in lower-risk segments.

title: Former Oklahoma Farmers Agent Accused of Sharing Confidential Data in Lawsuit
description: Former Farmers Insurance agent sued for alleged breach of appointment agreement and confidentiality, conspiring to move insureds to competitor agency. Addresses agent misconduct and client retention litigation risk.

title: After Milton’s Damage to Stadium, St. Pete Votes to Raise Coverage to $50M
description: St. Petersburg increases stadium insurance coverage to $50M post-Hurricane Milton damage, reversing prior cost-saving reduction. Reflects CAT event impact on municipal risk transfer decisions and adequacy challenges.

title: People Moves: TMK Appoints Glover From AIG as Aviation Underwriter
description: Tokio Marine Kiln appoints senior AIG aviation underwriter to expand aerospace division capacity. Reflects specialty underwriting talent mobility and market expansion in aviation risk.

title: US $20B Reinsurance Plan Unlikely to Restart Gulf Shipping Without Liability Cover
description: Trump administration's $20B maritime reinsurance facility for Strait of Hormuz shipping lacks liability cover, limiting effectiveness. Critical gap in P&I protection constrains war risk mitigation for Gulf shipping routes.

title: Encyclopedia Britannica Sues OpenAI Over AI Training
description: Encyclopedia Britannica sues OpenAI for alleged unauthorized use of reference materials in AI model training. Emerging technology IP infringement and cyber liability exposure for software platforms.

title: Judge Sides With Florida Citizens in Another Win for DOAH Arbitration Plan
description: Florida judge upholds Citizens Property Insurance arbitration endorsement constitutionality, supporting state insurer's dispute resolution framework. Strengthens Citizens' claims management and litigation avoidance strategy.

title: US Data Center Boom Slows Due to Power Grid Limits, Consultancy Says
description: US data center expansion slows due to power grid capacity constraints limiting facility development. Infrastructure underinsurance and business interruption risk emerging as grid constraints tighten.

title: US Charges Man Accused of Selling Gun to Old Dominion University Shooter
description: Federal charges filed against individual who sold stolen firearm used in Old Dominion University shooting. Highlights gun liability exposure and criminal negligence in weapons distribution networks.

title: TPG Launches Third Wave Brokerage, Which Acquires Palmer & Cay in Atlanta
description: TPG launches Third Wave Insurance brokerage and acquires Palmer & Cay in Atlanta through three-way deal. Reflects private equity consolidation in insurance brokerage sector and M&A activity.

title: Starbucks May Be Neglecting Labor Dispute Risks, Shareholder Proxy Firms Warn
description: Proxy advisory firms warn Starbucks underestimates financial and reputational risks from labor disputes. Corporate governance concerns highlight need for enhanced operational risk assessment and contingency planning amid ongoing union negotiations.

title: AIG, McGill Announce Collaboration to Potentially Transform Subscription Market
description: AIG partners with McGill to develop AI-driven subscription insurance platform for capital deployment. Initiative demonstrates digital transformation in underwriting distribution and potential disruption to traditional broker-insurer relationships.

title: Iran War Should Trigger Faster Exit From Fossil Fuel Dependence: UN Climate Chief
description: UN climate chief links Iran conflict to energy market disruption, advocating accelerated fossil fuel transition. Geopolitical volatility and energy market instability underscore climate-related insurance risks and energy sector exposure.

title: SiriusPoint Restructures Business, Now Operating Through Four Business Areas
description: SiriusPoint restructures operations into four business areas including Global P&C and Global Reinsurance. Organizational realignment suggests portfolio repositioning and strategic focus on specialty underwriting segments.

title: A Backlash Against Data Centers Is Spilling Into French Municipal Election Races
description: French municipal opposition to data center expansion highlights environmental liability and community risk exposure. Rising societal backlash against tech infrastructure creates underwriting challenges for facility operators and insurers.

title: Howden unveils regional structure changes, creates EMEA broking unit
description: Howden realigns International Growth Markets division by region, restructuring EMEA broking unit effective April 2026. Strategic reorganization indicates market consolidation and operational efficiency improvements across regional operations.

title: Markel International names Maureen Tomlinson as Head of AI and introduces AI Centre of Enablement
description: Markel International establishes AI Centre of Enablement with dedicated leadership. Investment in artificial intelligence capabilities signals industry-wide automation trends impacting claims, underwriting, and operational risk assessment methodologies.

title: MGA market share gains accelerate to ~7% in 2025: BMO Capital Markets
description: MGA market share accelerates to 7% in 2025 with $94 billion volume, representing 19% growth. Market concentration shift toward managing general agents indicates structural changes in P&C distribution and reinsurance placement dynamics.

title: Blenheim’s Syndicate 5886 reports strong 2025 results with £72m profit and 80.9% CoR
description: Blenheim Syndicate 5886 reports strong 2025 underwriting with 80.9% combined ratio and £72m profit. Lloyd's specialty syndicate performance demonstrates resilient underwriting discipline and profitable risk selection in competitive market.

title: UK pension schemes urged to review full range of endgame options: Aon
description: Aon advises UK defined benefit pension schemes to evaluate comprehensive endgame strategies amid market evolution. Pension risk management and scheme wind-down complexity require enhanced actuarial and liability risk assessment for insurers.

title: SiriusPoint announces new Global P&C Programs & London Market Specialty divisions
description: SiriusPoint restructures into four divisions: Global P&C Programs, Global Reinsurance, Global Accident & Health, and London Market Specialty including Lloyd's operations. Strategic realignment targets specialty market segments.

title: CEA’s total reinsurance limit rose to $7.912bn at year-end 2025
description: California Earthquake Authority's total risk transfer capacity increased to $7.912bn year-end 2025, combining $5.037bn traditional reinsurance with $2.875bn catastrophe bond protection. Strengthened CAT coverage.

title: Fitch downgrades ARC’s ratings on sustained earnings volatility
description: Fitch downgraded African Risk Capacity's Insurer Financial Strength Rating to BBB+ from A-, citing sustained earnings volatility and declining standalone credit quality. Credit deterioration impacts pan-African risk transfer.

title: McGill and AIG collaborate to transform subscription market with AI-driven underwriting
description: McGill and Partners partners with AIG for subscription market AI-driven underwriting platform. Agentic AI deployment enhances specialty underwriting capacity and client access to long-term insurance solutions.

title: Atrium appoints James Lakin as Head of Outwards Reinsurance
description: Atrium Lloyd's insurer appoints James Lakin as Head of Outwards Reinsurance. Leadership change focuses on strategic reinsurance programme design aligned with risk appetite and capital objectives.

title: Mideast war cuts Hormuz strait transit down to 77 ships
description: Strait of Hormuz transit volumes collapsed to 77 ships amid Middle East conflict escalation. Severe maritime route congestion creates elevated P&I, energy, and war risk exposure across global shipping operations.

title: Mideast war cuts Hormuz strait transit to 77 ships : maritime data firm
description: Maritime data confirms Hormuz Strait transits reduced to 77 vessels during Middle East hostilities. Significant shipping chokepoint disruption elevates underwriting risk across energy and general cargo portfolios.

title: Mideast war cripples Strait of Hormuz shipping , only 77 vessels cross in March
description: Hormuz Strait transit capacity severely constrained with only 77 ships crossing in March amid regional conflict. Critical chokepoint disruption drives elevated war risk premiums and supply chain insurance claims.

title: Greek Vessel Laden With Saudi Oil Sails Through Hormuz
description: Greek-flagged tanker carrying Saudi crude successfully transited Hormuz Strait, demonstrating continued but limited energy shipment movement. Individual vessel passage indicates selective corridor operations amid geopolitical constraints.

title: Grčki tanker uspio proći Hormuškim tjesnacem
description: Grčki tanker uspio proći Hormuškim tjesnacem. Greek oil tanker successful passage through Hormuz demonstrates restricted but operational energy transit despite regional instability and elevated marine risk environment.

title: Tanker uspio proći Hormuškim tjesnacem
description: Tanker transit operations through Strait of Hormuz facing operational challenges. Regional maritime chokepoint experiencing elevated vessel movement risks and potential passage disruptions affecting global energy logistics.

title: Attack on oil tanker  Skylight  highlights risks to Indian seafarers in Persian Gulf route
description: Oil tanker Skylight attacked in Persian Gulf, endangering Indian seafarers. Incident highlights escalating maritime security threats on critical shipping routes affecting crew safety and P&I exposures.

title: Iran may deploy naval mines in Strait of Hormuz , US intelligence believes
description: US intelligence reports Iran may deploy naval mines in Strait of Hormuz. Potential mine-laying operations would dramatically escalate transit risks and force rate adjustments on world's critical energy corridor.

title: US believes Iran may deploy mines in Strait of Hormuz
description: US intelligence assesses Iran potential naval mine deployment in Strait of Hormuz. Mine threat creates severe underwriting and claims exposure on tanker and general cargo transits through strategic waterway.

title: Shadow fleets of sanctions - skirting ships facing global crackdown : analysis
description: Sanctions evasion shadow fleet vessels face increased international enforcement actions. Unregulated tonnage circumventing compliance creates elevated claims frequency and insolvency risks across hull and liability accounts.

title: Three tankers damaged as Iran halts Hormuz traffic , Russia warns of global oil shock
description: Three tankers damaged; Iran restricts Hormuz traffic while Russia warns of global oil price shock. Major supply disruption event triggering marine casualty claims and commodity market volatility affecting energy sector exposure.
